issues.title: SelectableText and SelectionArea do not work correctly when calling document.execCommand("copy") unless you right-click
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

To work around some clipboard sandboxing issues when embedding DevTools inside VS Code, we have to detect `Cmd+C` and call `execCommand("copy")` for the selected contents to be copied to the clipboard.

In some cases this works, and in some other cases it does not.

At https://github.com/flutter/devtools/issues/9636#issuecomment-3936560379, @Renzo-Olivares noted that some of this handling involves text in a hidden element, but it is only updated when you right-click. This means if you do not right-click, then `execCommand("copy")` does not work, but if you do, then it works.

In the example video:

- I select the SelectionArea text (which is a `Text` inside a `SelectionArea`)
- I click the button that triggers `document.execCommand("copy")` (from `package:web`)
- I try to paste the contents, but nothing pastes
- Next, I select the SelectionArea text (which is a `Text` inside a `SelectionArea`)
- I right-click to show the context menu (but do not use it)
- I click the button that triggers `document.execCommand("copy")` (from `package:web`)
- I try to paste the contents, and now it pastes

Oddly, the SelectableText doesn't seem to work either way around, so I'm not sure if there is a separate bug there.

### Expected results

I expect `document.execCommand("copy")` to copy the selection to the clipboard.

### Actual results

Nothing is copied to the clipboard, unless I right-click first.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:web/web.dart' show document;

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Padding(
          padding: EdgeInsets.all(24),
          child: Column(
            crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
            children: [
              ElevatedButton(
                onPressed: () => document.execCommand('copy'),
                child: const Text('Trigger execCommand("copy")'),
              ),
              const SizedBox(height: 30),
              SelectableText('SelectableText'),
              const SizedBox(height: 30),
              SelectionArea(child: Text('SelectionArea')),
              const SizedBox(height: 30),
              TextField(decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Paste here')),
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

`execCommand("copy")` isn't working because the selection lives purely in Flutter's state, the browser has no knowledge of it.

What you need is to use a Flutter API that's aware of the current selection:

1. Get the selected content from `SelectionArea`.  Easiest way is to set an `onSelectionChanged` and keep track of the current selection. But this may not scale well if you have many `SelectionArea`, and I'm not sure if there's a cleaner way to listen for selection changes across the entire app. Any ideas @Renzo-Olivares ?

2. Call Flutter's `Clipboard.setData(ClipboardData(text: selectedText))`. Under the hood, this API uses the [`Clipboard.writeText()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Clipboard/writeText) provided by the browser.

@mdebbar I'm a bit confused, because it works fine if you just right-click first (I'm not using Copy on the context menu, I am simply right-clicking and then closing the menu). Based on my testing and @Renzo-Olivares 's comments at https://github.com/flutter/devtools/issues/9636#issuecomment-3936560379, it seems Flutter maintains a hidden input to support the platform-triggered copy.

@DanTup that's right. When you right-click, you trigger some [magic](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/c5d0a84f88bdbd29e22408a9353be0d1690b0872/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/_platform_selectable_region_context_menu_web.dart#L128-L137) that Flutter uses to prepare for the case that you click "Copy" in the context menu.

> Flutter is assuming that the platform-invoked copy only happens after a right-click, but this isn't always the case (for example the Edit -> Copy menu).

It's not about assumptions. It's about handling the most common scenario out of the box. Other use cases like `Edit -> Copy` or programmatically copying can't be detected beforehand, thus I'm not sure how we would implement it (we can probably have a permanent div and keep it always updated with current selection, but it may not be the most efficient solution).

> this feels like a bug that affects all apps and should be handled by Flutter?

I agree the current implementation is incomplete.

> in VS Code, the clipboard APIs are blocked, so calling them explicitly doesn't work.

So you are saying this [`Clipboard.writeText()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Clipboard/writeText) is unusable inside VS Code / Electron? Is this documented anywhere? I found the Electron [`clipboard`](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/clipboard) API, but I couldn't find anything about their blocking of the browser `Clipboard` API.

For your case, can you use the VS Code/Electron Clipboard API? It looks very similar to the browser Clipboard API.

> So you are saying this [Clipboard.writeText()](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Clipboard/writeText) is unusable inside VS Code / Electron? Not globally, just inside webviews with iframes in VS Code (see https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/182642). I've tried to debug this many times, but never figured it out - the permissions attributes all look like they should allow it, and it works in the outer frame - but not inside a nested iframe). > That's interesting! Is the `copy` event triggered when you use `execCommand("copy")` too? Yep (at least, in [my jsfiddle test](https://jsfiddle.net/bvtnfLc7/)): ```html

select this text and click

this text will be copied

``` > The reason we still need the right-click is to make sure the context menu shows the correct text Ah, yes - I thought it was just for the copy to work, but that makes sense - both would be needed.

Thank you for the investigation @DanTup and @mdebbar.

> Get the selected content from SelectionArea. Easiest way is to set an onSelectionChanged and keep track of the current selection. But this may not scale well if you have many SelectionArea, and I'm not sure if there's a cleaner way to listen for selection changes across the entire app. Any ideas @Renzo-Olivares ?

`onSelectionChanged` would be the most straight forward way to access the selected text. There is also `SelectionListener` which you can wrap either around the child of `SelectionArea` to listen to all selection changes (provides global offsets) in a `SelectionArea`, or you can wrap it around individual selectable widgets to listen to the local selection. With `SelectionListener` the user has to recreate the selected content based on the global selection offsets. Using either of these for this bug feels more like a workaround, for example you could capture the selected content in `onSelectionChanged` and have the keyboard shortcut copy that captured content.

The "copy" event is a great find! It seems reasonable to me to add a case to handle that, the hidden div element can listen for the copy event and update its selected content. Is this something either of you wanted to work on? If not i'll try to get to it soon.

>  Is this something either of you wanted to work on?

Sorry, I missed this question before.

I'm not familiar with this code, but it doesn't seem like it should be difficult, so I'll try and have a stab at it this week and post back if I get stuck. Thanks!

@Renzo-Olivares I started looking at this, but I'm having trouble getting the existing tests to run. I've tried both: ``` in packages/flutter: flutter test --reporter=expanded -v --platform=chrome --wasm .\test\widgets\selectable_region_context_menu_test.dart in dev/bots: $env:SHARD="web_skwasm_tests"; $env:SUBSHARD="0_last"; dart .\test.dart ``` In both cases, the browser opens to run the tests (I hacked headless=false in the code to debug), but trying to load `/canvaskit/skwasm` is a 404: If I try the canvaskit versions, canvaskit.js is also a 404. I'm trying to figure out where this is supposed to be built, but if you have any suggestions in the meantime, it might save me some effort. (I'm running on Windows, but I presume that's not the issue since I presume these tests run on the bots on Windows too) Edit: Maybe it is Windows? Looks like the same 404 showed up in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/162798. Edit2: Yup, seems to work on Mac, I'll work on it there for now.

@Renzo-Olivares I tried to look at this, but I think I'm in over my head 😄

At first I thought it would be a change in the widget (since the right-click code linked above was), but now I'm not so sure. I feel like there should be a single handle for the `copy` event on the document, that should just force an update of the hidden input to whatever the current selection is. But while trying to find where this hidden input is controlled I ended up in `engine/src/flutter/lib/web_ui/test/engine/text_editing_test.dart` (I think this is where the hidden input is created, since it's web-specific). But it's not clear to me how these two things interact.

I'm happy to have a go if you can provide some pointers (where you think the change would be, where the tests would live, and the best way to verify, since the clipboard APIs themselves seem blocked behind a prompt, so I couldn't use `web.window.navigator.clipboard.readText()` in a test for example), however if it would be easier for you to fix than hold my hands, that's fine by me too!

Thanks!

issues.title: [iOS] Physical keyboard input fails after virtual keyboard is hidden
issues.body:
I am currently developing a Flutter app which uses a bluetooth barcode scanner to input text in a text field. The bluetooth scanner is detected as a HID.

One of the constraints of this application is, that a user cannot enter a barcode manually using the virtual keyboard. To prevent this, I automatically hide the keyboard via `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide')` on focus gain.

On Android, this works without issues (Android 9, Samsung tablet).

On iOS, when a HID is connected, it shows what seems to be called `inputAccessory` when a text field is focused. I noticed that after hiding the keyboard/inputAccessory programmatically, there is no input detected from the barcode scanner anymore, even if the text field is still in focus.

Invoking `TextInput.show` restores functionality, but also (obviously) shows the inputAccessory again, which is undesirable.

I would accept this as an iOS specific quirk, however, when I hide the inputAccessory manually by tapping on its down arrow, it becomes hidden, but input from the scanner is still detected and put into the text field. In addition to this, I also noticed that after hiding the inputAccessory manually, I am unable to make it reappear by invoking `TextInput.show` again.

As I can reach the desired keyboard/inputAccessory state when hiding the inputAccessory manually, I assume there must be a way to achieve the same state programmatically. Is there a way to do this in Flutter?

Alternatively, is there a way to specifically only hide the virtual keyboard, but not the inputAccessory? I don't mind the inputAccessory bar, as long as I can prevent users from using the virtual keyboard to input barcodes.

If anyone knows of a solution or workaround, please let me know.

I'm attaching a two videos to illustrate the issue.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. focus a `TextField`
2. call `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide')`
3. scan a barcode / type something on a physical keyboard

Expected result: text should be put into text field
Actual result: no text input happening

## Videos

First is the flow as described in "Steps to Reproduce", i.e. focus a textfield, hide the keyboard/inputAccessory, scan an item: https://gfycat.com/rashoccasionalboilweevil

Second is about the quirk I mentioned, that initially I can hide and show the inputAccessory without issues, but if I hide the inputAccessory manually (by clicking on the arrow), I cannot reveal the inputAccessory programmatically anymore, but in this case the input of the scanner is respected and handled: https://gfycat.com/selfishmelodicindri

## Code
Here is the relevant code of an example app, the same you see in the videos:
```dart
class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  MyHomePage({Key key, this.title}) : super(key: key);

  final String title;

  @override
  _MyHomePageState createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  TextEditingController _textFieldController = TextEditingController();
  FocusNode _textFieldFocus = FocusNode();

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Column(
        mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
        children: <Widget>[
          Text(
              '1. click "request focus"\n'
              '(keyboard indicator should be visible)\n'
              '2. click "hide keyboard"\n'
              '3. scan a barcode / type something on the physical keyboard\n'
              'expected: text should be put into text field\n'
              'actual: no text input happening\n'
          ),
          TextField(
            onSubmitted: (text) => debugPrint('text submitted: $text'),
            controller: _textFieldController,
            focusNode: _textFieldFocus,
            autofocus: false,
          ),
          ButtonBar(
            children: <Widget>[
              RaisedButton(
                child: Text('request focus'),
                onPressed: () => _textFieldFocus.requestFocus(),
              ),
              RaisedButton(
                child: Text('hide keyboard'),
                onPressed: () =>
                    SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide'),
              ),
              RaisedButton(
                child: Text('show keyboard'),
                onPressed: () =>
                    SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.show'),
              ),
            ],
          ),
        ],
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

## Logs
I have tested multiple Flutter versions, but here is the log after trying it with the master branch state

issues.body:
Hello! I'm having a few issues going on in a mobile browser. When I open the application in a mobile browser iOS or Android during the scroll, some widgets disappear that are in the tree below the input elements. When you close the on-screen keyboard, an undefined area appears at the bottom of the screen, always the same height. Sometimes it appears when chaotically scrolling up or down after opening / closing the on-screen keyboard. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Execute `flutter run -d web-server --web-port 8080 --web-hostname 0.0.0.0` on the code sample 2. Copy the address and open it in a mobile browser 2. Click on any input field to display the on-screen keyboard 3. Try scrolling up or down 4. Close the on-screen keyboard 5. @fulstadev 
This is producing expected results, keyboard resizes the `CupertinoTabScaffold ` and  your `ContainerWithBG` widget wraps the `CupertinoTabScaffold`, Its size changes as `CupertinoTabScaffold` resized.  This should produce with Material `Scaffold`. 

As you mentioned you don't wanna set `resizeToAvoidBottomInset`  to `false`. I recommend using a `Stack` widget to make sure `ContainerWithBG` stays the same size.

IMO, this is not a bug. If you need help please refer to Stackoverflow.

@TahaTesser I've been trying it for hours with `Stack` too, with and without posts on SO. The result is exactly the same as the one mentioned: it works on the base level, but not for app screens that are normally pushed. Feel free to prove what you say: Are you able to show a working example with `Stack` where the bottom bar stays and the background image won't resize when the keyboard is toggled on a normally pushed screen with a `CupertinoTabScaffold`? 

If not, this issue effectively prevents you from using a background image in an app using `CupertinoTabScaffold` on normally pushed screens, I do consider this as a bug, or at least, as a problem. It makes it impossible to use a background image across your Flutter App if that App uses normal push navigation and a `CupertinoTabScaffold` which IMO is a very basic requirement..?

@fulstadev 
I took a look at your sample. It looks like when you do "regular" push you're nesting the `UpperLayer` inside the parent `CupertinoTabScaffold`,  which is resized when the keyboard is shown. The "regular" push in the `CupertinoTabScaffold` is expected to shift the pages rather push new page.

When you do `rootNavigator` push, this replaced the parent `CupertinoTabScaffold` and pushes a new and you're not nesting `UpperLayer`, hence no the background stays the same. You can see parent `CupertinoTabBar` is shifted away do this.

If you don't specify `resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false`, `CupertinoTabScaffold` will resize.  
`Stack` might not help here, however this will surely require updating your code, your `UpperLayer` nested in the parent `CupertinoPageScaffold` will resize.

comments:
Thanks for the report @lukaszdebowski 
I used the code sample provided and ran it on latest stable and beta using iOS  device running on 15.3.1, and was able to see the system prompt upon tapping `next` button as expected:

In your video, I see that after inputing email and password each, you are tapping on `done` button and then on `next` button, but I didn't try that way, as the natural thing is to tap in next input field to make it active and then tapping on `next` button. Without additional information, we are unfortunately not sure how to resolve this issue. We are therefore reluctantly going to close this bug for now.
If you find this problem please file a new issue with the same description, what happens, logs and the output of 'flutter doctor -v'. All system setups can be slightly different so it's always better to open new issues and reference the related ones.
Thanks for your contribution.

So you did exactly the thing I described as "iOS prompt to save the password appears after the password input loses the focus". 

The point of the bug is that the prompt should be shown upon calling `TextInput.finishAutofillContext()`, and that is not the case - the prompt is not shown when someone calls that method, and instead it is only shown after the password input loses focus

IIRC there's no public APIs on iOS to trigger the "save password prompt", UIKit does that automatically when the view gets removed from the view hierarchy. 
For that reason "save password" is only shown when you navigate away from the register screen.

> In my case, navigating to previous screen (i.e say user doesn't want to login anymore or go to forget password page) shows the save password prompt.

Ah interesting. I don't think the Flutter framework knows whether the user is heading to the "forget password" page (so `finishAutofillContext(save: false)` should be called) or has successfully logged in (`finishAutofillContext(save: true)`) should be called, so for now the app developers have to call `finishAutofillContext(save: false)` themselves when the user navigates away from the login/register screen without successfully logging in. 

I'll file a new issue for a better API, but for now `finishAutofillContext(save: false)` needs to be called to prevent the save prompt to show up.

You can solve it by using alignLabelWithHint: true inside the inputDecoration

```
TextFormField(decoration: InputDecoration(alignLabelWithHint: true, labelText: 'Password',hintText: "Insert your password"))
```

@TahaTesser I added `isDense: true` to the `decoration` of the `TexField` and then customized the paddings with `contentPadding`. Like so:

```dart
TextField(
	...,

	decoration: InputDecoration(
		...,

		isDense: true,
		contentPadding: const EdgeInsets.all(8.0),

		...,
	),
	
	...,
),
```

issues.title: Flutter add-to-app fragment inserts unnecessary bottom padding when keyboard is open
issues.body:
Internal: b/258263557

## Description

If a flutter fragment is imported into an android app & the fragment is not full screen (e.g. a screen with bottom navigation bar), opening a keyboard adds an additional padding in flutter.

![Android without flutter](https://i.stack.imgur.com/Gptqdm.png)
![Flutter fragment in android with keyboard closed](https://i.stack.imgur.com/Qpau0m.png)
![Flutter fragment in android with keyboard open](https://i.stack.imgur.com/0rluDm.png)

Bigger images

 1. https://i.stack.imgur.com/Gptqd.png
 1. https://i.stack.imgur.com/Qpau0.png
 1. https://i.stack.imgur.com/0rluD.png

Image three shows the padding between the keyboard and the text field. It is clearly coming from flutter (as it is white). It is always the same height, as the distance from the end of the fragment to the end of the screen.

We know of `resizeToAvoidBottomInset`, but using this will cut off text fields and won't scroll the active one into the view, which is what we want.

**I assume it is a bug in the flutter framework, can someone confirm that?**

## Steps to Reproduce

A minimal working example on [github](https://github.com/DarkMikey/flutter_add_to_app_bottom_padding_issue/tree/main) was created. 
It includes a small flutter fragment + an android example (File > New > New Project > Bottom Navigation Activity).
I followed the instructions provided by the flutter docs

 - [Integrate a Flutter module into your Android project](https://docs.flutter.dev/development/add-to-app/android/project-setup)
 - [Adding a Flutter Fragment to an Android app](https://docs.flutter.dev/development/add-to-app/android/add-flutter-fragment)

**Expected results:** 
No padding between keyboard & fragment. Flutter should not insert any padding if the keyboard of the host app is open(?) 

**Actual results:** 
Whitespace between keyboard and padding when flutter fragment is used in android & iOS and keyboard is open.

same on ios. how to fix?

issues.title: Hardware keyboard handlers are invoked when TextField keyboardType is set to number, phone, dateTime etc. on mobile device.
comments: I think you simply use the wrong widget for that task. Did you try [`KeyboardListener`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/KeyboardListener-class.html)?

> I think you simply use the wrong widget for that task. Did you try [`KeyboardListener`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/KeyboardListener-class.html)?

I think you're missing the issue. I'm listening to the hardware keyboard for a Bluetooth controller. That's working. 
The issue is that using a TextField with the virtual keyboard type set to number while listening to the hardware keyboard will result in the hardware keyboard listener being invoked when tapping a digit on the virtual keyboard.
This doesn't happen when the virtual keyboard type for the TextField is set to text.
The behaviour is inconsistent between the types.

Thanks for the report @JaseElder 
I verified this using latest stable and master running on pixel emulator and observed that upon textfield getting focus, but we don't get to input anything from the virtual keyboard, but is the expected behavior that the input should be visible using virtual keyboard ?

@darshankawar Yes that's right - thats the visible behaviour. Or rather, the input not being visible is a symptom of the hardware keyboard listener firing on virtual keybord key presses, with a side effect being that the input is not visible in the TextField. 
So the expected behavior would be that the hardware keyboard listener should not be invoked on a virtual keyboard.

Hey @dkwingsmt, any idea off the top of your head why these keyboardTypes wouldn't trigger the ServicesBinding.instance.keyboard listener?

I'm surprised that the listener is triggered at all, since `HardwareKeyboard` is supposed to work for hardware keyboards and not software keyboards.

cc @gspencergoog to see if it rings a bell.
The `triaged-design` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-design` label or `fyi-design` label.

> I'm surprised that the listener is triggered at all, since `HardwareKeyboard` is supposed to work for hardware keyboards and not software keyboards.


That's exactly the issue I'm reporting. The title has been changed erroneously.
Adding applicable labels.
Ah you're right, my bad for changing the title!

Unfortunately, mobile platform "keyboards" aren't actually hardware keyboards, they're GUI touchscreen apps that send text editing events to the app from another process (essentially, they are input method editors). Some keyboards simulate hardware keyboard events, but some do not, and they don't have any requirement to do so.

Because of this, and because it's not possible to convert text editing commands into a deterministic stream of simulated keyboard events, pressing buttons on a software keyboard doesn't always generate hardware key events, and we don't have control over when it does and doesn't happen (it's up to the implementer of the keyboard).
Sounds like this is working as intended then... Maybe we can add something like @gspencergoog's comment above into the docs if anyone thinks it would help?

I agree that it's a fundamental issue, but it's not one we have control over, unfortunately.

Sure, we probably should document this better. Where in the documentation did you first look when you wanted to find out what was going on? (I'll make sure that place at least has a pointer to the right documentation)

I didn't - I first thought it was something I was doing, and then came here to see if there was an already existing issue before logging my own.
I guess, because it only surfaces when using **both** the `TextField keyboardType` and the `ServicesBinding` hardware keyboard listener, it should go in `packages/flutter/lib/src/services/hardware_keyboard.dart`, somewhere around the `addHandler()` method code?

Anybody knows solution? 

Iam using 
`HardwareKeyboard.instance.addHandler(keyEventHandler);`
and when numeric keyboard is used, it detects events. In previous versions of Flutter I used RawKeyEventDataAndroid and checked if deviceId is not -1. That is not possible now.

> pressing buttons on a software keyboard doesn't always generate hardware key events

Ok, but if flutter editable detects the button and actually changes text controller value why aren't we able to do the same?

> Would like to add some information about the issue for those, who also affected. I believe that if the project has minSDK higher than 19 `webview_flutter` **BEFORE 3.0.0** version will not use hybrid composition despite readme file claiming that hybrid composition is turned on by default.
> 
> I work on a project where I changed minSDK to 21 a few weeks ago (earlier had 19) and users with Android 12 started reporting that keyboard doesn't appear for them. What helped me is forcing hybrid composition manually:
> 
> ```
> @override
>   void initState() {
>     if (Platform.isAndroid) WebView.platform = SurfaceAndroidWebView();
>     super.initState();
>   }
> ```
> 
> Only after this change keyboard started to appear on Android 12 devices as well as the exception mentioned in the issue disappeared.
> 
> So those affected should check that their `flutter_webview` version is updated to `3.0.0` or use the code I provided.
> 
> The reason I can't update to 3.0.0 and not use it? Because of dependencies like `flutter_html`, which aren't compatible with `flutter_webview: 3.0.0` yet.

The entire `WidgetSpan` is treated as a single character by the Flutter engine's text APIs.

In the example app, the `TextEditingController` value contains the 6-character string "widget".  But when the `EditableText` calls the text positioning APIs exposed by the `TextSpan` with the embedded `WidgetSpan`, those APIs think that the corresponding region only consumes one position in the text.  This mismatch will cause problems for the text editor.

A while ago there was an attempt to build a generalized API for this use case but it looks like it never landed (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/80185)

For now, one potential way to implement this would be overriding the `value` setter in `WidgetSpanTextFieldController` to replace the "widget" substring in the text with a single placeholder character.  The offsets in the value's `TextSelection` would also need to be adjusted.

`buildTextSpan` can then substitute the placeholder character with a `WidgetSpan`.  The one-to-one mapping between the placeholder's position in the `TextEditingValue.text` and the `WidgetSpan`'s position in the `TextSpan` should allow the text editor to function as intended.

> The entire `WidgetSpan` is treated as a single character by the Flutter engine's text APIs.
> 
> In the example app, the `TextEditingController` value contains the 6-character string "widget". But when the `EditableText` calls the text positioning APIs exposed by the `TextSpan` with the embedded `WidgetSpan`, those APIs think that the corresponding region only consumes one position in the text. This mismatch will cause problems for the text editor.
> 
> A while ago there was an attempt to build a generalized API for this use case but it looks like it never landed (#80185)
> 
> For now, one potential way to implement this would be overriding the `value` setter in `WidgetSpanTextFieldController` to replace the "widget" substring in the text with a single placeholder character. The offsets in the value's `TextSelection` would also need to be adjusted.
> 
> `buildTextSpan` can then substitute the placeholder character with a `WidgetSpan`. The one-to-one mapping between the placeholder's position in the `TextEditingValue.text` and the `WidgetSpan`'s position in the `TextSpan` should allow the text editor to function as intended.

Is there a way someone could implement this for an arbitrary number of unique WidgetSpans? For example, say you have a list of names that may grow from user input. What would some ideal candidates for the single placeholder text value, and could there be a way to generate unique placeholders for each unique name? 

I originally thought you meant extending PlaceholderSpan to replace the substring, then to replace that extended PlaceholderSpan with the intended WidgetSpan. But that's really just confusing the steps. Could you use unicode values as the unique characters? Or is there some way to create some sort of text value replacing object that can carry data which allows creating the intended WidgetSpan?

In the case of unicode (forgive my lack of knowledge) it seems that they would not be able to serve as a proper replacement for a single value of text. So what would be a good example of a single placeholder character that is not likely (or impossible) to be inputted by a user?

Alternatively, would extending WidgetSpan somehow allow changing what the text editing API's expect the position to be to instead be a range?
Hi @GaryQian , https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/83537 says there might be a following-up PR to deal with selection/caret positioning. Just curious what is the priority for that?

We never landed the framework "replacements" API (#80185), which seems to be exactly what you are looking for. The API is a bit too use case specific for something as general as widgetspans. However, as Jason mentioned, the essence of the code is to insert a zero width placeholder character in place of the widget to act as a stand-in such that the caret is offset properly.

You are welcome to explore the unlanded code and create a custom version of it to use for you app, however, it is currently not within our roadmap to provide this functionality in the near future.

This issue is assigned but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!

issues.title: [proposal][desktop] Tab character (\t) should be displayed as indentation in TextField
issues.body:
## Tab Character \t should be displayed as indentation in EditableText, TextField and all implementations of Text widgets in Desktop
#### This is a common behaviour in many desktop applications

Flutter Channel: **master**
Platform: **Desktop, Linux**
Version: **2.1.0-11.0.pre.138**

When Tab key is pressed while the TextField is focused, in a Flutter Desktop Application, by default, the focused TextField is unfocused and the focus shifts to another widget. That is a problem in my case as I want the user to be able to enter tab characters. But I partially solved it by using a RawKeyboardListener to detect when the user presses Tab key, and then inserted a \t to the TextField at the current cursor position through its controller.
The problem is that, the \t character is displayed as a small space, almost half as wide as whitespace, which is counterproductive.
There should be an option to configure how \t is displayed, as it can be of multiple number of spaces' width, and it should be customisable.

I believe this is not exactly a bug. For mobile devices, the Tab key is irrelevant. For web, pressing Tab key is helpful in shifting focus between UI elements.
But for desktop, it makes sense to have an option to customise how a Tab character is displayed in a TextField.

### Suggestion
Add an option to disable focus shifting on Tab press on Desktop and add Tab character to the TextField. Provide a way to customise Tab character display, by equating it to a specific number of whitespaces that can be changed if required.
comments: Yes this is definitely a proposal. Just tested on Slack MacOS app and Tab button move focus to another component.

@TimWhiting @pedromassangocode 

I tested the implementation by Tim Whiting and it works well. :+1: 

However, I reviewed the code and found that it is adding the appropriate number of spaces whenever the user presses Tab key. I have already implemented this in the application that I am developing. Is there anyway that we can add the '\t' character in the text and make it look like multiple spaces? I mean, without replacing the tab with spaces?
In theory, we should be able to configure and customise the display of certain characters in the text field. Especially when we consider the possibility that desktop applications developed by flutter might not always be mere "applications", they might be complex enough to be called "software".
### Use case
I want the user to be able to add the raw tab character (\t) to the text field. And I want to customise the display of the tab character to resemble the equivalent number of spaces chosen by the user.
I am developing a text/code editor. I think you get where I am going with this. If a user/programmer presses tab to indent their content/code, and if I write code to replace the indentation with actual whitespace, someone's definitely getting angry. I can easily provide them with an option to replace '\t' with spaces, but that shouldn't be the default behaviour in this case.

Yeah, I agree, the properties should be more like:
- bool acceptTabs (with this property set to false which should be the default pressing tab should change focus)
- int indentSpaceCount (this property controls the visual spacing of a literal \t,  default should probably be 4)
- bool replaceTabsWithSpaces (this property controls whether to replace a literal \t with indentSpaceCount of spaces, default should be false) or there should be an InputFormatter provided by Flutter to do the same

As a design note, for accessibility there needs to be a way to unfocus the field that accepts tabs to a larger focus scope to allow moving to a different field. I believe this is typically done by the escape key, but I could be wrong.

@TimWhiting

Exactly, this model looks perfect. And yes, Escape key is usually the way to do it.

Thinking a bit more:

The `indentSpaceCount` should probably be in the `TextStyle` instead. Which can be specified application wide.

And `replaceTabsWithSpaces` should not be a property and users should use a `TextInputFormatter` instead to replace \t with whatever so that users can do anything (for example code editors should indent the start of the line the user pressed \t on, and not at the location they pressed it, same for bulleted lists).

So the `TextInputFormatters` that should be added would be:
`IndentLineFormatter()` --> replaces a \t anywhere in the line with a \t at the beginning of the line
`ReplaceTabFormatter(int numSpaces)` --> replaces a \t with numSpaces of plain spaces
Which could be chained to provide both.

Also I forgot in my implementation to add a Shift-tab which would need a
`DedentLineFormatter(int numSpaces)` --> replace a \t or numSpaces at the beginning of the line with ''

Edit:
But I guess this is where the TextInputFormatters break down, because any time it formats it will remove another \t or spaces until everything is dedented completely. So maybe the Shortcuts / Action framework is really the better fit for handling tab / shift-tab. But we could still use TextInputFormatters to do the job to allow for flexibility for the user to change tabs to whatever they want. But instead of have them in the list of general TextInputFormatters, they should be two properties:
- tabFormatter
- shiftTabFormatter

or:
- indentFormatter
- dedentFormatter
- tabFormatter (indentFormatter would be used if it is not null, otherwise tabFormatter would be used if it is not null)

I agree that there should be something like a `tabWidth` in the TextStyle, that controls how wide a tab is drawn when rendered, the units should probably be in multiples of the em space of the font. There should be a property on the text field that says whether it handles tabs or not.  Replacing tabs with spaces is probably something that should be done after the text is received by the app, since that's highly dependent on the use case (a rich text editor would want tab stops, a code editor might want to start at a particular initial indent before replacing tabs with spaces, etc.)

Note that if a text field handles tabs, then the app is no longer traversable via the keyboard, since as soon as a text field gets focus, it will not allow the focus to leave again when the user presses tab. This is bad for accessibility on all platforms (including mobile platforms), which is why the current implementation always interprets tabs as focus change events. @TimWhiting in your usage of the escape key to enable this, what is the behavior? Does it just jump to the next control? Does Shift-Esc do anything (like go to the previous control)?

We aspire to someday be able to allow people to easily write a code or rich text editor in Flutter, but we're definitely not there yet, and this is one of the areas where we're missing functionality.

I've thought before that there should also be some way to configure all of the text fields in a widget subtree based on the `TextInputType` of the field (and maybe allow defining user types for text fields), which would be useful in configuring more complex apps for things like this.

cc @HansMuller

@gspencergoog 

I have implemented many major features required for a basic code editor in my application. The application can autosave files and create new files. it supports basic auto-completion. It starts with Dark Mode as the default colour scheme. It can generate a Syntax Tree for the code as the user types. Git support, Opening files within app and custom terminal commands is in the prototype phase, and custom file save is already implemented. I have even devised a logic to implement a custom file browser, since the default flutter package is not supported in all platforms. All of these features are using just Dart and Flutter. No other programming language is used. The app is in private development for now, and it will be released to the public once all major features are implemented and tested.
**Handling of the Tab character is the only important thing that I find to be missing right now. If there already is a way to implement this using `TextInputFormatter`, that will be great. As far as I know, there isn't, but may be I haven't looked in the right place.**
***As of now, all I need is to control the display of the \t character. In theory, the `TextInputFormatter` should be able to handle it, as it seems like it was made for purposes like this.***

@TimWhiting 
Is there anyway to implement this without the use of a predefined Formatter? Does the `TextInputFormatter` support low-level customisation like that?

According to the `TextInputFormatter` docs: A `TextInputFormatter` can be optionally injected into an `EditableText` to provide as-you-type validation and formatting of the text being edited.

So it can format / allow characters / disallow characters, alter text (replace \t with two / four spaces). But it is not meant for controlling how characters are displayed. As @gspencergoog mentioned, visual tab width for display should probably be a property on `TextStyle`, but is not possible now. I suspect that will involve some engine work.

@TimWhiting 

Oh ok then. Thanks for the helpful response. It seems like something like this won't be implemented in the flutter engine for a long time. So for now and the foreseeable future, Tab characters in the TextField in my application will look like half-spaces. That's really counterproductive. And unfortunate. At least, it will still be Tab characters when the user saves the file.

You might be able to create some sort of workaround for now without engine changes, but it will probably be more complex. I'm really not sure what is required to actually display tabs better, maybe the engine is not actually involved.

@TimWhiting 

I had a brief look at:
**https://pub.dev/packages/flutter_multi_formatter**

and its Github Repository:
**https://github.com/caseyryan/flutter_multi_formatter**

But it seems to be a predefined static spacing between two characters and doesn't seem to be dynamic.

@AldrinMathew 
Yeah formatters aren't what you are looking for:

@gspencergoog 
I've implemented two approaches to the tab character / tab focus problem:

Approach 1: Escape to exit entering tabs into the textfield and move to next field. Shift escape to exit entering tabs and move to previous field
Approach 2: Tabs aren't automatically captured when textfield becomes focused until user presses enter, escape exits the tab entering mode but still has focus on the textfield, as a part of the regular tab order. (There is an 'outer' and 'inner' hierarchical focus, that you navigate using enter / escape).

2 seems like the better approach and feels more natural since you can tab right past the field without automatically getting captured. Also probably fits best with voice/accessibility interfaces. The shift-escape-tab solution feels awkward, but tabbing to the previous field is probably less used.

Here is the code:
https://github.com/TimWhiting/test_app

And the demo app on github pages: https://timwhiting.github.io/test_app/#/

@TimWhiting 

I tested the Test App and the second implementation (***Escape** and **Enter** to exit and enter TabScopes*) makes more sense. It is not much of hindrance to accessibility, and changes focus only when required and not automatically.

@TimWhiting

The current implementation in my code editor changes the focus every time the user presses Tab key and only refocuses on a second key press. *Do I have the permission to use the implementation of Shortcuts & IndentationAction in your *Test App* in my application?*

Yeah you have permission. It's meant to be a sample to help solve this issue and determine what the desired behavior is. There might be better ways of doing it. Hopefully something like it will be integrated into the framework someday.

Another conflicting use-case of Tab in text fields is for tab auto-complete. It seems to me that this can be distinguished from a insert of a literal tab by first checking if there is a suggestion available and the selection is empty, if so, then the first suggestion should be applied. If there is no suggestion available or the selection is not empty than the tab should be taken as a literal tab.
I've created a design doc here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1aHucsI0NWGWu2Dm_XFsBLxiTgJRM8h2XBB7PVAnVxlU/edit?usp=sharing&resourcekey=0-zLbXFlP_A2e_Yoi43vdiiw
Any news about this topic?
@TimWhiting The design doc looks like it's on the right track to me, are you still working on bringing it to Flutter?  Happy to help if needed.

@justinmc I was waiting for feedback on the design doc and for all of the changes around TextEditing / Shortcuts / Actions that were happening at the time, and since then I have had some other priorities. I'm busy with a bunch of stuff right now for my Master's Thesis, so would welcome any contribution or help you have to offer. If you want to fold these changes into your text editing refactor that would be great!
Sorry I thought I had already replied here.  I will see if I can get this in as part of my current text editing work.  Thanks for the great design doc and good luck on your thesis!

issues.title: TextEditingController is not firing event when it already has a value on "From Mail" is isued
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Receive a One Time Password through email
2. Start typing in your input field to add one or more characters
3. Then use the smart sugggestion that is shown above the iOS keyboard that proposes to autofill the code (in our case 6 characters)
4. Nothing happens, but the code from the email is shown under quotes as the first suggestion in the iOS keyboard hint bar.

### Expected results

I would expect that the "From Mail" functionality resets the TextEditingController and fires the event with the value. 

### Actual results

The TextEditingController does not fire any change. Debugging the code, I found that neither `set text(String newText)` nor `set value(TextEditingValue newValue)` are called.

### Code sample

Unfortunately I don't know how to provide a sample that allows to mock the "Fill from Email" hint on iOS.
